journal_entry.rationale: Customer previously paid a $100 deposit creating a liability (unearned revenue). Now that the service has been delivered, we decrease the liability (debit Unearned Revenue) and recognize the revenue earned (credit Revenue). No tax applies as the deposit was already collected with tax at the time of initial receipt, and this entry only recognizes the revenue component.

output.reason: Customer deposit is a liability (unearned revenue). When the service is delivered, the liability decreases (debit the deposit liability) and revenue is recognized (credit revenue). Credit side: Revenue increase = 1 line in slot c.

credit_classifier.reason: Customer deposit is a liability (unearned revenue). When the service is delivered, the liability decreases (debit the deposit liability) and revenue is recognized (credit revenue). Credit side: Revenue increase = 1 line in slot c.

output.reason: When service is delivered against a customer deposit, the deposit liability (unearned revenue) is converted to earned revenue. Debit: Liability decrease (customer deposit/unearned revenue). This reduces the obligation to the customer as the service has now been performed.
